item.title: Denote.nvim
item.description: Note taking for me was very hard for a long time. I thought maybe something like obsidian will help me but I use neovim and didn’t really wanted to use something else. So after some thinking I figured I can create neovim plugin for that. Overview First concept of tags, next installation and configuration, then basic usage using Denote user command and Finally api usage of denote.nvim. I will use v0.
item.content:encoded:
Note taking for me was very hard for a long time. I thought maybe something like obsidian will help me but I use neovim and didn’t really wanted to use something else. So after some thinking I figured I can create neovim plugin for that.

Overview

First concept of tags, next installation and configuration, then basic usage using Denote user command and Finally api usage of denote.nvim. I will use v0.1.0 tag in this article. Let us begin.

Concept of Tags

Tags allow you to group your ideas together. For example you have a project and have some related notes. You can have a tag designed just for that.

Installation and configuration

Install it with Your package manager of choice. I use lazy.nvim so here is snippet for that:

{
    "HumanEntity/denote.nvim",
    tag = "v0.1.0",
    cmd = "Denote",
    build = "mkdir -p ~/.denote",
    opts = {}
}

The opt field is our configuration. Here is default one:

{
	filename = {
		ext = "norg",
		date_sep = "_",
		name_sep = "-",
		tag_sep = "=",
	},
	vault = {
		dir = "~/.denote/",
	},
}

Here is a little breakdown

  • filename.ext is the extension for notes.
  • filename.date_sep is separator for date and name.
  • filename.name_sep is separator for name and tags.
  • filename.tag_sep is separator for tags.
  • vault.dir is the directory where notes will be stored.

Usage

The usual way (probably) you gonna interact with denote.nvim is using Denote user command. For now you can create and search for notes but first we need to create some notes.

Note creation

To create note use :Denote note and it will prompt you for name and tags. Name is just string and tags a space separated list. Don’t want any tags just press enter. It will create file with file name in this format %Y%M%D__name--tag1=tag2. The default format is norg.

Searching for note

To search for note use :Denote search, it will prompt you for date in %Y %M %D format, name, and tags in first second third format. It will use you will get list of items matching it.

Conclusion

After my struggle with note taking I have found something that fits my needs. It’s easy to use and isn’t bloated. Thanks for reading.
